filter_index.pickup_city: Sheikh Feroze Tours & Travels, Sathu, Barbara Shah Chowk, Srinagar
filter_index.video_title: Pir Panjal Lakes Trek | The Grandest Trek In Jammu & Kashmir | Indiahikes

avoid_months: All other seasons outside end-June to start of September
snow_or_rain_notes: Upper reaches under heavy snow until mid-June; lower meadows open earlier
End-June to mid-July: remnants of snow at highest lake (Nandansar), melting ice floating in lake
Mid-July onwards: snow has almost melted
Mild rainfall in Jammu during monsoon due to Pir Panjal range blocking most rain clouds
Evening showers pick up from mid-July onwards
temperature_notes: Mid-July to start of September: Day 20-25°C, Night 2-4°C
Cold sets in as season progresses to end of August and start of September
Post mid-September: cold temperatures make it less comfortable

terrain_challenges: Long trekking days of 7-8 hours almost every day
Unmarked trail over big boulders and loose rocks at Khabi ki Gali
altitude_or_weather_risks: Day 1 altitude gain of more than 3,000 ft from ~6,000 ft to ~10,000 ft increases susceptibility to Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS)
Above 10,000 ft, AMS risk is real for both first-time and experienced trekkers
Even fit trekkers can develop AMS if they climb too high too soon
Best done in monsoon when meadows and lakes are at their best
Heavy rain can flood campsites and make forest trails slushy

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 5 km in under 32 minutes
approval_requirements: Fitness approval from the Indiahikes team is required 20 days before the trek date
Without fitness approval, trekkers will not be allowed on the trek
At least 3 runs from the last 1 month must meet the fitness criteria
Use apps like Nike or Strava to record runs
Paste a public profile link on the Indiahikes account
prep_window: 6 to 8 weeks before the trek begins
6-week fitness plan available for currently inactive trekkers

experience_themes: End-June to mid-July: remnants of snow at highest lake Nandansar, melting ice floating in lake, lower meadows open earlier, upper reaches under heavy snow

Mid-July to start of September: bright green meadows carpeted with wildflowers (violet, purple, yellow, white), deep blue alpine lakes (royal-blue shade), evening showers, brilliant blue skies reflecting in lakes, meadows turning golden brown by end of August/start of September, flowers wilting
Rugged grey mountains rising at fringes contrasting with bright green meadows
Alpine lakes with signature royal-blue shade (Nandansar, Laksukhsar)
Brilliant blue skies reflecting in lakes when clouds part
Meadows turn golden brown by end of August/start of September
primary_watchouts: 3-4 warm layers required throughout the season
Evening showers become frequent as season progresses
Cold increases towards end of August and start of September
Post mid-September: cold temperatures reduce comfort
Trekkers visiting post mid-September due to cold temperatures
Trekkers visiting in seasons outside end-June to start of September (too cold, snow-buried trails and meadows)
best_for: Trekkers seeking comfortable monsoon-season trekking with mild rainfall
Trekkers wanting to see wildflower-carpeted meadows (mid-July onwards)
Trekkers wanting to see snow remnants and melting ice at Nandansar (end-June to mid-July)
Trekkers wanting to see deep royal-blue alpine lakes
not_ideal_for: Trekkers visiting post mid-September due to cold temperatures
Trekkers visiting in seasons outside end-June to start of September (too cold, snow-buried trails and meadows)

video_title: Pir Panjal Lakes Trek | The Grandest Trek In Jammu & Kashmir | Indiahikes
